skill.name: nextjs-app-router-patterns
skill.description: Apply proven Next.js App Router patterns for Server Components, streaming, routing, caching, metadata, and Server Actions.
skill.summary: Guides Next.js 14+ App Router architecture with practical Server Component and routing patterns.
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: Static command execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance findings are false positives caused by Markdown fences, TypeScript examples, ordinary metadata fields, and routing identifiers. No prompt injection or malicious runtime behavior was found in SKILL.md, but one instructional API route example writes a parsed request body directly to the database without validation or authorization context.

medium_findings.title: Instructional API Route Omits Validation
medium_findings.description: The route handler example parses request JSON and passes the body directly into a database create operation. This is not malicious skill behavior, but users could copy a pattern that lacks schema validation, authorization, and field allowlisting.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code example clearly shows request.json() followed by db.product.create({ data: body }). It is an instructional security concern rather than evidence of malicious execution by the skill.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution locations align with Markdown code fences and TypeScript code blocks. No shell command, Ruby interpolation, child process call, or executable script is present in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line-number review shows fenced Markdown and TypeScript examples at every reported command location. There is no executable file or instruction that asks the agent to run these snippets as shell commands.

low_findings.title: Network and Environment Access Are Example Code
low_findings.description: The fetch calls, hardcoded documentation links, and process.env.API_URL reference are part of Next.js teaching examples. They show ordinary application data fetching and configuration, with no evidence of credential exfiltration or hidden outbound behavior by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network and environment references appear only inside explanatory TypeScript snippets or resource links. I found no endpoint receiving secrets and no instruction to execute network requests during skill use.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography and Reconnaissance Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography flags correspond to ordinary descriptive text and metadata fields. The reconnaissance flags correspond to route parameters, identifiers, or rendering examples, not host inspection or system discovery commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reviewed locations contain Next.js prose, metadata description properties, route params, or database update examples. No cryptographic primitive, hash operation, uname, whoami, environment dump, or filesystem reconnaissance appears.

dangerous_patterns.title: Raw Request Body Used For Database Create
dangerous_patterns.description: An API route example forwards parsed request JSON directly into a database create call. Safer guidance should validate the body, authorize the caller, and allowlist fields before persistence.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is explicit in the example and can produce insecure application code if copied unchanged. It does not indicate malicious intent by the skill author.

content.user_title: Build Better Next.js App Router Apps
content.value_statement: Next.js App Router projects can be hard to structure when routing, streaming, caching, and Server Components interact. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical patterns for modern Next.js application architecture.
actual_capabilities: Explains App Router file conventions such as layout, page, loading, error, route, template, and default files.
Provides patterns for Server Components, Client Components, Server Actions, and route handlers.
Shows streaming approaches with Suspense boundaries and independent loading states.
Covers parallel routes, intercepting routes, and modal routing structures.
Summarizes caching options including no-store, force-cache, revalidation, and tag invalidation.
Demonstrates metadata generation and static parameter generation for SEO-focused pages.
limitations: It is a Markdown guidance skill, not a runnable Next.js project generator.
Examples use placeholder functions such as db, getProduct, and processOrder.
Some code samples omit production checks such as authentication, schema validation, and field allowlisting.
It focuses on Next.js 14+ App Router and does not cover the legacy Pages Router in depth.

use_cases.title: Plan a New App Router Project
use_cases.description: Choose file conventions, rendering modes, loading states, and route boundaries before implementation starts.

use_cases.title: Modernize Existing Next.js Routes
use_cases.description: Translate Pages Router habits into App Router patterns with Server Components, layouts, and route handlers.

use_cases.title: Review Architecture Patterns
use_cases.description: Check whether a feature uses caching, Suspense, metadata, and Server Actions in a maintainable way.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Rendering Pattern
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Next.js App Router patterns skill to recommend rendering modes for this page. Include Server Component, Client Component, static, dynamic, and streaming tradeoffs.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Route Structure
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the skill to design an App Router folder structure for this feature. Include layout, page, loading, error, route handler, and default files where appropriate.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Data Fetching
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the skill to review this data fetching flow. Recommend caching, revalidation, tag invalidation, and Suspense boundaries for a Next.js App Router implementation.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Advanced Routing
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the skill to evaluate this App Router design with parallel routes, intercepting routes, Server Actions, and metadata generation. Call out security and validation gaps.

output_examples.input: A product listing page needs filters, pagination, and fast first paint.
output: Recommendation: keep the page as a Server Component and fetch filtered products near the list.
Use Suspense around the product list so filters and surrounding layout can render first.
Use tag-based revalidation for product data and validate query parameters before database access.

output_examples.input: A dashboard needs independent analytics and team panels.
output: Recommendation: use parallel routes for panels that load independently.
Give each panel its own loading state so slow analytics do not block team data.
Keep shared navigation and layout state in the parent dashboard layout.

output_examples.input: An API route accepts product creation requests.
output: Recommendation: parse the request, validate it with a schema, authorize the caller, and allowlist fields.
Return clear status codes for validation errors and missing records.
Avoid passing the raw request body directly into persistence calls.

best_practices: Start with Server Components and add Client Components only for browser APIs, hooks, or direct interactivity.
Place Suspense boundaries around slow data areas so the rest of the page can stream quickly.
Validate request bodies, form data, route params, and search params before using them in database operations.
anti_patterns: Putting use client at the top of large route trees without a clear interaction requirement.
Passing raw request bodies directly into database writes or Server Actions.
Using dynamic rendering for content that can be cached, prebuilt, or revalidated safely.

faq.question: Does this skill generate a complete Next.js application?
faq.answer: No. It provides architecture guidance and code patterns that an agent can apply inside an existing project.

faq.question: Which Next.js versions does it target?
faq.answer: It targets Next.js 14 and later with the App Router model.

faq.question: Can it help with Server Components?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains when to use Server Components and when a Client Component is needed.

faq.question: Does it cover caching?
faq.answer: Yes. It describes fetch caching, revalidation, tags, and path invalidation patterns.

faq.question: Is the skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a medium-risk note. The skill is guidance-only, but one API example should be improved before users copy it.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, and Claude Code use it?
faq.answer: Yes. The metadata lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
